defining moment Zimbabwean constitutional reform came 2008 after disputed violence riddled elections 2008 when three main political parties entered into transitional Government National Unity were tasked with establishment constitution which ultimately adopted 2013 following protracted turbulent process which began 2009.
Some segments civil society however argued that concerned political parties 'captured constitutional project narrowed short-term struggle motivated pursuit party political interests expense will people nation's broad long-term interests', thereby subverted and/or negated aspirations people.
against this background that study therefore assesses participation, role significance rural populace drafting 2013 Zimbabwean Constitution.
study traces history constitutional reform efforts Zimbabwe, beginning with colonial Lancaster House Constitution 1979, protracted exercise 2009 2013 which gave birth current Constitution.
focuses 2009-13 constitution making process case study.
study employs interdisciplinary approach adopting both doctrinal empirical research approaches.
study employed doctrinal research approach provide doctrinal analysis relevant global, regional domestic legislation case law.
empirical research approach, through interviews, used collect qualitative data from general members rural populace institutions such political parties human rights organisations from three selected rural districts, namely Bulilima, Makonde Mutasa.
study indicated that while significant number rural populace participated constitution making process, legal environment which subsisted during constitution making process allow unfettered flow information ideas, direct result repressive legislation such AIPPA, Criminal (Codification Reform) Act, Interception Communications POSA, among others similar laws, well deeply polarised political environment owing nature relationship between ZANU PF-led government opposition political parties.
study further showed that process heavily dominated political parties Global Political Agreement political parties wanted ensure adoption constitution that best reflected their preferences partisan views rather than will masses, making 2013 Constitution elitist negotiated document, contrary provisions Article which provided right Zimbabweans make constitution themselves themselves.

2018 Berlin anti-semitic attack attack street German capital Berlin.
young wearing Jewish skullcaps were insulted Arabic-speaking passers-by.
two, Israeli citizen, beaten with belt Palestinian attacker.

April 2018, Adam Armoush, 21-year-old Israeli Arab living Berlin German friend Moroccan origin went through street Prenzlauer Berg taking selfies while wearing kippahs.
According Armoush, trying argument with Jewish friends Israel proving that could walk through Berlin wearing Jewish skullcap.
point, argument with three Arab-speaking young started, which followed violent attack.
Armoush managed document violence with cell phone later allowed video shared Facebook, from where video went viral.
video shows main attacker, later identified 19-year-old Palestinian from refugee camp Syria come Germany refugee, beat Armoush repeatedly with belt.
During onslaught, attacker shouted word "Yahudi", Arabic word Jew, before companions intervened dragged away.
According Armoush, main attacker also tried hurt with glass bottle.
consequence beating, Armoush suffered minor injuries.
Before turning himself police, main attacker contacted representative Palestinian student community Berlin appeared short video that distributed social media.
They denied hostile towards Jews that actions been motivated anti-Semitism.
main attacker turned himself accompanied lawyer after incident.
arrest warrant against executed.

Israeli television, Merkel warned persistent hatred Jews Germany.
spoke "different form anti-Semitism" coming from people Arab descent.
After this other incidents, President Central Council Jews Germany, Josef Schuster, warned Jewish "not show themselves openly with kippah metropolitan milieu Germany".
Under slogan "Berlin Wears Kippah" Jewish community Berlin called citizens wear kippah April solidarity with victims.
